Off Calendar Sep-30-2020 Text Ruling

Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Nicole S. Dunn
Off Calendar Sep-18-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Magdalena Apostolova (415-218-4939) Plaintiff in propria persona.
Jonathan Fink (949-477-5050; jfink@wrightlegal.net) for Defendants.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT WILMINGTON SAVINGS FUND SOCIETY, FSB's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions:

The motion is unopposed and will be granted except as to the matter of sanctions. Plaintiff shall file code-compliant further responses within 30 days after the entry of this order. The term "code-compliant" means in compliance with the California Civil Discovery Act, Code of Civil Procedure sections 2016.010 et seq., as interpreted by the decisions of the courts of this state, including, without limitation, the case decisions cited in defendants' moving papers.

The court finds plaintiff's responses to be a misuse of the discovery process for which sanctions may be awarded. Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 2023.030. It is no excuse that plaintiff is not represented by counsel. "[T]he rules of civil procedure must apply equally to parties represented by counsel and those who forgo attorney representation." Rappleyea v. Campbell, 8 Cal.4th 975, 984-85 (1994); see Robert J. v. Catherine D., 171 Cal.App.4th 1500, 1527 (2009) (holding "pro per litigants are held to the same standard as parties represented by trained legal counsel").

The court assesses sanctions against plaintiff in the amount of $2406 but withholds imposition thereof pending further order of the court. Plaintiff is informed that avoidance of the imposition of the sanctions is dependent upon plaintiff's timely service of code-compliant further responses. Plaintiff is further informed that additional sanctions for further abuses of the discovery process include, without limitation, striking the complaint, dismissal of the action, entry of judgment by default and contempt. Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 2023.030.

Prevailing/Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling via email to jameshfleming@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Plaintiff Magdalena Apostolova'S Motion To Consolidate Actions Of Case(S) Cud-18-662196, Cud-18-662562 With Case Cgc-20-583201
Off Calendar Jul-24-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Magdalena Apostolova'S Motion To Consolidate Actions Of Case(S) CUD-18-662196, CUD-18-662562 With Case CGC-20-583201 is ordered off calendar. The parties agree that the latter two cases are on appeal; this trial court thus lacks jurisdiction over them. (CCP 916.) A consolidation motion is not an exception to the 916 rule. (Witken, California Procedure (5th ed. 2008) 17, 20.)

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
